mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production over 2 months signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% rise in housing starts over 2 months signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over 3 months and a decline in industrial production by > 10% over 2 months signals a weakening labor market and demand for goods and services that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% over 3 months and INDPRO falls by > 10% over 2 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment and declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production over 2 months and a 5% rise in housing starts over 2 months signals a weakening demand for goods and services and an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by > 10% over 2 months and HOUST rises by > 5% over 2 months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% rise in housing starts over 2 months signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
